analysis: Hotspot is the memory-bandwidth-bound SAXPY loop (z = a*x + y). The selected plan reduces memory traffic by using 32-bit floats for x/y/z and scalar a, while keeping checksum accumulation in double for stability. Change is localized to array/scalar types and initialization/casting; alignment via aligned_alloc(64, ...) is preserved by rounding allocation size up to a multiple of 64 bytes to satisfy C11 aligned_alloc requirements for the new element size.
selected_candidate_target: SAXPY arrays x/y/z and scalar a in the memory-bandwidth-bound loop
applied_changes_summary: Converted x/y/z arrays and scalar a from double to float; updated initialization and SAXPY loop to use float arithmetic; updated checksum routine to accept float input while accumulating in double; adjusted aligned_alloc sizes to be multiples of 64 bytes for correctness.
correctness_risks: Numerical differences due to single-precision storage/arithmetic may change CHECKSUM vs double baseline beyond acceptable tolerance for some use cases
If n*sizeof(float) is not rounded to a multiple of alignment, aligned_alloc would be undefined; this is mitigated by rounding the allocation size up
expected_metrics: Reduced memory bandwidth demand (half the bytes moved for x/y/z), improving runtime on memory-bound systems
Improved cache behavior (lower cache miss rate / better effective bandwidth)
